programme.title: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew explores how Charles I's Royal Collection introduced a new artistic language to British art. The sensuality of Titian and the epic canvases of Tintoretto, still in the Royal Collection today, were a revelation for a country whose visual culture had been obliterated by the Reformation.

programme.title: Spirit of Yachting
programme.desc: First held in 1953 and run every year since, the Giraglia Rolex Cup is one of the oldest and most prestigious events in the yachting calendar. It is a glorious spectacle as, after three days of inshore racing, the fleet sets sail from world renowned St Tropez towards the titular Giraglia rock. This year the event breaks with tradition by then heading towards a finish in Monaco, a suitably glamorous climax to a week's magnificent sailing.

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: Winter 2020 sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a groundbreaking new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin alongside paintings by her great hero Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum in spring 2021.

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: Winter 2020 sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a groundbreaking new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin alongside paintings by her great hero Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum in spring 2021.

programme.title: Click
programme.desc: Click hits the racetrack to meet a motorsport team with a difference. Team Brit is made up of six disabled drivers using bespoke, specially adapted cars to compete in races against non-disabled drivers on a level playing field. And Spencer takes a high-speed ride with Sam Schmidt, a former Indycar racer, paralysed in a crash, who has developed technology which allows him to race again.

programme.title: Wonders of Greece
programme.desc: Bettany travels to Aigai in the heart of Northern Greece, a capitol of the ancient Kingdom of Macedon, which boasts the biggest palace of all classical antiquity and one of the most spectacular archaeological treasure troves ever discovered. Bettany then visits Nafplion, one of Greece's most romantic cities and its first official capitol city. She finds that it is a place that embodies the compelling, complicated history of Greece.

programme.title: Wonders of Greece
programme.desc: Bettany travels to Aigai in the heart of Northern Greece, a capitol of the ancient Kingdom of Macedon, which boasts the biggest palace of all classical antiquity and one of the most spectacular archaeological treasure troves ever discovered. Bettany then visits Nafplion, one of Greece's most romantic cities and its first official capitol city. She finds that it is a place that embodies the compelling, complicated history of Greece.
